heading: Service Accounts Overview
body:
Service accounts are the recommended method to manage authentication to
the Atlas Administration API. Service accounts provide improved security over
API (Application Programming Interface) keys by using the industry standard [OAuth 2.0](https://oauth.net/2/) protocol with the
[Client Credentials flow](https://www.oauth.com/oauth2-servers/access-tokens/client-credentials/).

A service account lets you manage permissions and create access tokens
that authenticate API (Application Programming Interface) requests. Each service account has a client ID
and a secret that function as a username and a password for creating
access tokens. To learn how to construct an API (Application Programming Interface) request with an access
token, see [Make an API (Application Programming Interface) Request](example-api-request).

> **Note:** [IP Access List](access-list) apply to *using* service account access tokens, not
> [cloud.mongodb.com/api/oauth/token](generate-oauth2-token-atlas) or [revoking](revoke-oauth2-token-atlas) them.
> You can generate a token from any IP address, but you can only use it to call
> the API (Application Programming Interface) if your IP address is on the access list.

Access tokens are only valid for 1 hour (3600 seconds) per the
[OAuth 2.0](https://oauth.net/2/) specification. This limited lifespan protects against replay
attacks, where an attacker uses a leaked access token without time
restriction.

Each service account belongs to exactly one organization, and you can
grant it access to any number of projects within that organization. To
give an organization-level service account access to a project, see
[Grant Existing Service Account Access to a Project](invite-org-app-api-keys).

Atlas [Atlas User Roles](user-roles) limit which operations a service
account can authenticate with its access token. You must assign roles to
service accounts as you would for users to ensure that the service
account generates access tokens with the necessary permissions for the
desired API (Application Programming Interface) calls.

You can't use a service account or its access token to log into
Atlas through the Atlas UI. Service accounts only grant access
to the Atlas Administration API, which does not include UI access or access
to cluster data. To learn more about the limitations of service
accounts, see [MongoDB Limits and Thresholds](manual/reference/limits).
